fianna-fail.title: Affordable Housing Levy enacted
fianna-fail.summary: Fianna Fáil pushed through the Affordable Housing Act, introducing a new levy on land zoned for housing to deter hoarding and release more sites for development.

fianna-fail.title: Rural broadband milestone: 1m premises connected
fianna-fail.summary: The National Broadband Plan, championed by Fianna Fáil ministers, passed the one-million-premises connected milestone, bringing high-speed fibre to rural Ireland.

fianna-fail.title: €500m Agriculture Resilience Fund agreed
fianna-fail.summary: Following CAP reform negotiations, Minister for Agriculture secured a dedicated €500 million resilience fund for Irish farmers facing income volatility.

fianna-fail.title: Nursing home support scheme expanded
fianna-fail.summary: Fianna Fáil secured an expansion of the Fair Deal nursing-home scheme, capping the contribution rate and making it accessible to more families.

fine-gael.title: Corporation tax deal: 15% global minimum agreed
fine-gael.summary: Fine Gael negotiated Ireland's entry into the OECD Pillar Two global minimum tax framework, retaining significant flexibility for SMEs while satisfying EU partners.

fine-gael.title: Garda recruitment surge: 1,000 new officers sworn in
fine-gael.summary: Under Fine Gael's justice programme, the Garda College swore in its largest cohort in a decade, targeting 15,000 sworn officers by end of term.

fine-gael.title: IDA record inward investment year
fine-gael.summary: The IDA reported a record €14 billion in approved FDI projects, with Fine Gael's business-friendly tax and regulatory environment cited as a key factor.

fine-gael.title: Children's Hospital opens first wards
fine-gael.summary: After years of delays, the National Children's Hospital opened its first wards to patients, with Fine Gael claiming credit for the final funding push.

sinn-fein.title: Cost-of-living motion carried: energy credit extended
sinn-fein.summary: Sinn Féin's Dáil motion calling for a further extension of the household energy credit secured enough cross-party support to pass, forcing government action.

sinn-fein.title: Vacancy tax amendment adopted by government
sinn-fein.summary: After sustained pressure from Sinn Féin, the government adopted an amended version of their vacancy tax proposal to penalise long-term derelict properties in cities.

sinn-fein.title: All-Ireland economic forum established
sinn-fein.summary: Sinn Féin's long-standing proposal for a formal North-South economic forum was agreed by both governments, marking a major step toward cross-border planning.

sinn-fein.title: Windfall tax on energy profits passed
sinn-fein.summary: Following Sinn Féin's sustained campaign, a temporary windfall solidarity tax on energy company profits was enacted to fund cost-of-living relief.

green-party.title: Climate Action Plan 2025 targets confirmed on track
green-party.summary: The EPA confirmed Ireland's 2025 sectoral emission ceilings are on track, crediting measures enacted under the Green Party's stewardship of the Climate Action and Low Carbon Development Act.

green-party.title: BusConnects Cork approved
green-party.summary: Following Dublin, the Green Party-backed BusConnects redesign was approved for Cork, promising a 25% increase in bus frequency across the city.

green-party.title: Offshore wind auction sets record
green-party.summary: Ireland's second Renewable Electricity Support Scheme auction awarded 2.4 GW of offshore wind capacity, the largest single clean-energy procurement in Irish history.

green-party.title: Single-use plastics ban extended to foodware
green-party.summary: The Green Party secured regulations extending Ireland's single-use plastics ban to takeaway cups and containers, ahead of the EU implementation deadline.

labour.title: Living wage enacted in law
labour.summary: Labour's long-running campaign succeeded: the Low Pay Commission's living wage recommendation became a statutory floor, lifting the minimum wage above €14/hr.

labour.title: Statutory sick pay extended to 10 days
labour.summary: Labour-backed legislation extended mandatory employer-paid sick leave from 5 to 10 days, matching the European norm and covering 2.3 million workers.

labour.title: Right to request remote work strengthened
labour.summary: Amendments championed by Labour closed loopholes in the Work Life Balance Act, giving workers a stronger legal basis to seek remote-work arrangements.

labour.title: Public dental scheme extended to under-25s
labour.summary: Labour secured the extension of free public dental care to all under-25s, building on the Sláintecare roadmap.

social-democrats.title: Dáil lobbying register reform enacted
social-democrats.summary: A Social Democrats Private Members Bill strengthening the lobbying register — requiring real-time disclosure and closing the 'revolving door' loophole — was adopted by the government.

social-democrats.title: Cost-rental housing scheme scaled up
social-democrats.summary: Following Social Democrats pressure, the government tripled the annual target for cost-rental homes, directly delivering below-market rents to middle-income households.

social-democrats.title: GP visit card extended to all under-18s
social-democrats.summary: After years of Social Democrats advocacy, free GP visits were extended to all children under 18, ending the income-threshold restriction.

social-democrats.title: Second public university for Dublin approved
social-democrats.summary: The Social Democrats' proposal for a new technological university specifically serving the Dublin commuter belt was approved in principle in the Programme for Government.
